note: Per-card VRAM mode: start.sh exports CUBRIC_VRAM_MODE (>=32GB normalvram else lowvram); wrapper _build_cmd uses self.vram_mode. Co-ships sage in v0.10.0. Self-verified.

LIVE BUG: 5090 (32GB) reports total_memory as 31GiB (nvidia usable, under 32) -> my start.sh threshold >=32 picks --lowvram, should be --normalvram. Same rounding made 4090 read 23 not 24. FIX: lower the normalvram cutoff to >=28 (clean split: 24GB->23, 32GB->31; 28 separates them). One-number edit in start.sh, rides next rebuild. This Pod ran lowvram so the normalvram path is NOT yet live-tested.

5090 (cu128) HUNG: RAM 97% (83.72/85.68GiB), VRAM 2%, util 0% — GPU never loaded. CAUSE = the 31GiB<32 threshold bug forced --lowvram on the 5090, so lowvram streamed the 42GB transformer+15GB encoder into 85GB system RAM -> OOM-thrash/hang. This 32GB card SHOULD run --normalvram (model resident in VRAM, NO RAM streaming) and would not OOM. The threshold bug DIRECTLY caused this. NOTE: sage-on-Blackwell STILL UNTESTED — the gen died at RAM-load before sampling, so sage kernels never ran on cu128. Need the threshold fix (>=28) THEN a fresh cu128 retest to actually test sage on Blackwell + normalvram.
